dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6283 IH: Furthering Opioid Services, Training, and Education Resources Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Strickland (for herself and Mr. Fitzpatrick) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend the 21st Century Cures Act to support State and local agencies with responsibility for children services in their response to the opioid abuse crisis, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Furthering Opioid Services, Training, and Education Resources Act or the FOSTER Act.

section.header: Opioid grants to support caregivers, kinship care families, and kinship caregivers

subsection.text: Section 1003(b)(4) of the 21st Century Cures Act (42 U.S.C. 290ee–3 note) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating subparagraph (F) as subparagraph (G); and
by inserting after subparagraph (E) the following:

subparagraph.text: Supporting opioid abuse prevention and treatment services within a State provided by State and local agencies for children and caregivers, kinship care families, and kinship caregivers through—

clause.text: health care services (including such services described in subparagraph (D)); and
foster and adoptive parent recruitment and training.

subsection.text: Section 1003 of the 21st Century Cures Act (42 U.S.C. 290ee–3 note) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating subsections (h), (i), and (j) as subsections (i), (j), and (k), respectively; and
by inserting after subsection (g) the following:
The term kinship care family means a family with a kinship caregiver.
The term kinship caregiver means a relative of a child by blood, marriage, or adoption, who—

subparagraph.text: is the primary caregiver of the child because the biological or adoptive parent of the child is unable or unwilling to serve as the primary caregiver of the child; and
has a legal relationship to the child or is raising the child informally.

subsection.text: Paragraph (1) of section 1003(j) of the 21st Century Cures Act (42 U.S.C. 290ee–3 note), as redesignated by subsection (b), is amended by inserting , and $255,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2028 through 2033 after 2027.
Section 1003(j)(3)(B) of the 21st Century Cures Act (42 U.S.C. 290ee–3 note), as redesignated, is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking , and up to and inserting , up to; and
by inserting before the period at the end , and 1 percent of such amount for such fiscal year shall be made available to carry out subsection (b)(4)(F).
